# PDF Sign

> Chrome extension for signing, filling, and editing PDFs entirely inside the browser. Files never leave the user's computer. Free for the first signed file; Pro at $2.99/month or $29.99/year for unlimited.

## Overview

PDF Sign is a Chrome Web Store extension (Manifest V3) that lets users:

- Draw signatures with a trackpad or mouse, save them, and reuse across documents
- Fill PDF form fields — text, checkboxes, dates
- Overlay text in 5 fonts (Helvetica, Times, Courier — Latin only; Roboto and Open Sans — Latin, Cyrillic, Greek)
- Process files up to 50 MB
- Work offline after install (except at download, which makes a single backend call to verify sign-in and increment the usage counter)

All PDF reading, editing, and writing runs inside the browser tab using pdf-lib (write) and pdfjs (read). Nothing is uploaded.

## Privacy

The backend stores only:

- A Google sign-in identifier
- A per-account counter of signed files (enforces the free-tier limit)
- Subscription status (Free or Pro)

The backend never sees:

- The PDF content
- The values typed into forms
- The signature image

Privacy policy: https://molchanik.github.io/pdf-sign-extension/privacy-policy.html

## Pricing

| Plan        | Price        | Quota                                  |
| ----------- | ------------ | -------------------------------------- |
| Free        | $0           | 1 signed file total per Google account |
| Pro monthly | $2.99 / mo   | Unlimited                              |
| Pro yearly  | $29.99 / yr  | Unlimited (17% off monthly)            |

Billing runs via Stripe through ExtensionPay. Users can cancel at any time from the extension options page; the Pro plan stays active until the end of the paid period.

## Signature type

PDF Sign produces a **Simple Electronic Signature (SES)** as recognised under ESIGN (US) and eIDAS (EU). This is the legally appropriate level for everyday leases, offers, NDAs, and internal forms.

PDF Sign is NOT:

- eIDAS Advanced Electronic Signature (AES)
- eIDAS Qualified Electronic Signature (QES)
- DocuSign, Adobe Sign, or any qualified Certificate Authority workflow

Those require a Certificate Authority and a cryptographic time-stamp, which PDF Sign does not ship.

## Authentication

Sign-in uses `chrome.identity` with Google OAuth. Sign-in is requested only at the download step — users can open the editor, load a PDF, draw signatures, and fill fields without an account. The Google account picker is native to Chrome (no redirect to an external tab).

## Install

- Chrome Web Store: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/jeaailkfagoabacaflpdhppafmabonmp
- Landing page: https://pdf-sign-extension.pages.dev/landing/

## Support

Email: pdfsign.support@gmail.com
